title: eucryphia milliganii subsp. pubescens
intro: Eucryphia milliganii subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy silverwood, is a subspecies of the Eucryphia milliganii plant, which is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand. The hairy silverwood has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of the hairy silverwood include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically used to support respiratory health and relieve colds and flu symptoms.
